response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist software developer (10+ years) but is a poor fit for this specialized embedded security role. The position requires native-level Japanese, which the resume does not indicate (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German). C proficiency for UNIX development is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments years ago, and there is no evidence of the core domain skills: LAN-switch firmware, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Layer-2/3 networking, or Linux kernel source-code investigation and modification. The bulk of the experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang).
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience across many domains and 10+ years
Some prior C/C++ work (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments) and Linux familiarity
Strong testing/craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling)
concerns: No native-level Japanese, which is a hard requirement
No embedded networking, switch firmware, or security-protocol (MACsec/802.1X) experience
No demonstrated Linux kernel source-code modification or strong recent C for UNIX

key_factors.factor: Weak/dated C-for-UNIX and no kernel work
